engineIntelligence.h2: Ferrari 550 575m Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the main engines fitted to the Ferrari 550 and 575m in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data.

cards.altText: Ferrari 550 575m F133B engine - petrol 5.7L replacement UK
knownFailures: Timing chain tensioner collapse - hydraulic units fail around 60k miles causing chain skip.
Oil pump drive gear shear - high-RPM use can shear stock gears leading to sudden oil loss.
Camshaft variator wear - bronze bushings wear causing rough idle and rattling noise.

cards.altText: Ferrari 550 575m F133A engine - petrol 5.5L replacement UK
knownFailures: Cylinder head cracking at exhaust ports - thermal cycling causes fractures requiring dye-penetrant verification.
Early hydraulic tensioner failure - pre-1999 units prone to collapse without upgraded mechanical kits.
Rear main seal leaks - common on higher-mileage examples requiring Viton seal upgrades.

cards.altText: Ferrari 550 575m F133D engine - petrol 5.7L replacement UK
knownFailures: Cylinder head cracking - same exhaust port vulnerability as F133A/B requiring MLS gasket upgrades.
Carbon-fibre intake manifold inspection - rare but critical check during rebuilds for Superamerica units.
VANOS solenoid calibration - cold-start hesitation requires ISTA+ remapping during rebuild.
cards.replacementCost: Typical UK rebuilt engine: £14,200-£14,500 (supply only) Estimated from comparable F133 units - actual quotes may vary due to Superamerica rarity.

entries.title: 5.5L Petrol
entries.history: Original 550 Maranello powerplant; aluminium block with Nikasil liners, defined Ferrari's front-engined V12 revival.
compatibleVariants: 550 Maranello (all trims), 550 Barchetta Pininfarina (F133E detuned calibration)
commonFailures: Cylinder head cracking at exhaust ports - thermal cycling fractures documented on high-mileage units
Hydraulic timing chain tensioner collapse - pre-1999 units require mechanical upgrade kit
Rear main seal leaks - Viton seal upgrades recommended during rebuilds
entries.cta: Get quotes for F133A Ferrari 550 575m engine replacement

entries.title: 5.7L Petrol
entries.history: Displacement increase for 575M; revised oiling system and updated ECU mapping for improved drivability.
compatibleVariants: 575M Maranello (standard), 575M Maranello F1, 575M GTC (F133C dry-sump variant)
commonFailures: Oil pump drive gear shear under high-RPM use - billet steel upgrades prevent sudden failure
Camshaft variator bronze bushing wear - causes rough idle, addressed via Ferrari service bulletin F133B-04-01
VANOS solenoid cold-start hesitation - requires ISTA+ recalibration during rebuild

entries.title: 5.7L Petrol (Superamerica)
entries.history: Exclusive to limited-run Superamerica; carbon-fibre intake and titanium retainers for 540hp output.
commonFailures: Cylinder head cracking - same exhaust port vulnerability as F133A/B requiring MLS gasket upgrades
Carbon-fibre intake manifold inspection - rare but critical check during Superamerica rebuilds
Fuel rail sensor ethanol corrosion - UK E10 fuel compatibility requires ethanol-resistant sensor upgrades

entries.title: 5.7L Dry-Sump Petrol (GTC)
entries.history: Race-derived dry-sump lubrication for 575M GTC; scavenge pumps prevent oil starvation during track use.
compatibleVariants: 575M GTC (competition-only, not road-legal in standard form)
commonFailures: Scavenge pump wear under sustained high-RPM - track-use validation required during rebuilds
Dry-sump oil change interval critical - 100-mile intervals with Fuchs Titan recommended for competition use
Timing chain tensioner upgrade mandatory - mechanical kits essential for race-spec reliability

commonProblems.h2: Ferrari 550 575m Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Ferrari 550 and 575m's F133 V12 engines are robust but known for three critical failure modes: cylinder head cracking, timing chain tensioner collapse, and oil pump drive shear. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address these original failure points - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use the cost comparisons below to decide whether repair or replacement offers better value for your specific variant.

problems.h4: Cylinder Head Cracking - Why Ferrari 550 575m Engines Fail at Exhaust Ports
problems.affectedModels: Ferrari 550 Maranello, 550 Barchetta, 575M Maranello, 575M Superamerica (1996-2006, F133A/B/D)
problems.rootCause: Thermal cycling causes micro-fractures at exhaust port junctions; exacerbated by aggressive driving or missed coolant service.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Dye-penetrant testing, weld repair of minor fractures, MLS gasket upgrade
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if cracks are superficial; not suitable for through-port fractures

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete long-block rebuild with crack-tested heads, upgraded MLS gaskets, bench dyno validation, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended for through-port cracks or high-mileage engines; prevents recurrence with upgraded components

problems.recommendation: On a Ferrari 550 or 575m valued at £80,000-£150,000, cylinder head cracking is rarely economical to repair in isolation. We recommend a full rebuilt engine with crack-verified heads and MLS gasket upgrades - it addresses the root cause and provides warranty protection that spot repairs cannot match.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt F133 engine prices for your Ferrari 550 575m

problems.h4: Timing Chain Tensioner Failure - Why 575M Engines Skip at 60k Miles
problems.rootCause: Hydraulic tensioners lose pressure with age; chain skip causes valve timing loss on this interference engine.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove front cover, replace hydraulic tensioners with mechanical upgrade kit, new chain and guides
repairOptions.longevity: Preventative fix if caught before skip; not viable if valves have already contacted pistons

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuild with mechanical tensioners pre-fitted, valve train inspection, compression validation, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if chain skip has occurred; rebuilt units include upgraded tensioners as standard

problems.recommendation: If your 575M shows chain rattle but no misfires, the intermediate tensioner upgrade is cost-effective preventative maintenance. However, if the engine has skipped or shows compression loss, replacement with a rebuilt unit including mechanical tensioners is the only reliable solution - and often more economical than repairing valve damage.
problems.cta: Get quotes for 575M timing chain tensioner upgrade or replacement

problems.h4: Oil Pump Drive Shear - Sudden Failure Risk on High-RPM 575M Use
problems.typicalFailureMileage: Any mileage under sustained high-RPM or track use
problems.rootCause: Stock oil pump drive gears can shear under high load; causes immediate oil pressure loss and catastrophic engine damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove oil pan, replace stock gears with hardened billet steel drive, pressure test
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable as preventative maintenance before failure occurs

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuild with billet oil pump pre-fitted, full bearing inspection, dyno validation, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Required if pump has sheared; rebuilt units include upgraded pump as standard

problems.recommendation: Oil pump shear is catastrophic and cannot be repaired after failure. If your 575M is used for track days or high-RPM driving, we strongly recommend preventative billet pump installation. If failure has already occurred, a rebuilt engine with the upgraded pump is the only viable path forward.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt F133B engine prices with oil pump upgrade included

problems.h4: Camshaft Variator Wear - Rough Idle and Rattling on 575M F133B
problems.rootCause: Bronze bushings in variators wear prematurely per Ferrari service bulletin F133B-04-01; causes rough idle and timing noise.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove cam covers, replace variator bushings per bulletin, recalibrate VANOS solenoids
repairOptions.longevity: Effective fix if caught early; does not address other potential F133B wear items

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuild with upgraded variators pre-fitted, full valve train inspection, ISTA+ calibration, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended for high-mileage examples or if multiple wear items are present

problems.recommendation: Variator wear is a known, fixable issue on the F133B. If rough idle is your only symptom and compression is good, the intermediate variator rebuild offers excellent value. However, if your 575M has other high-mileage wear signs, a full rebuilt engine with upgraded variators and comprehensive warranty may provide better long-term peace of mind.
problems.cta: Find a reconditioned Ferrari 575M engine with variator upgrade included

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Ferrari 550 575m
finalCta.paragraph: The F133 V12 is a magnificent engine, but its known failure modes demand proactive management. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Ferrari 550 575m specialists who understand these specific issues. Every rebuilt engine we supply includes upgraded components addressing cylinder head cracking, tensioner collapse, and oil pump shear - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare prices today and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Ferrari 550 575m Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Ferrari 550 or 575m and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Ferrari 550 575m Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Ferrari 550 and 575m is listed below, with compatible F133 engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify the right replacement.

items.title: Ferrari 550 575m 5.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 5.5L F133A powered the original 550 Maranello and limited Barchetta, establishing Ferrari's modern front-engined V12 formula with aluminium construction and Nikasil liners for reduced weight and improved thermal management.
commonFailurePoints: Cylinder head cracking at exhaust ports; early hydraulic timing tensioner collapse pre-1999

items.title: Ferrari 550 575m 5.7L Petrol Engines
items.description: The enlarged 5.7L F133B debuted in the 575M with revised oiling, updated ECU mapping and optional F1 automated manual; later variants added dry-sump lubrication for GTC competition use and carbon-fibre intake for Superamerica.
commonFailurePoints: Oil pump drive gear shear under high-RPM; camshaft variator bronze bushing wear; VANOS solenoid calibration requirements

items.title: Ferrari 550 575m 6.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 6.0L F133 GT was developed exclusively for 575 GTC competition use; features dry-sump lubrication, race-spec internals and 605hp output - not road-legal in standard form but highly sought for track programmes.
commonFailurePoints: Scavenge pump wear under sustained high-RPM; dry-sump service interval criticality; timing component stress under race conditions
items.cta: Enquire about Ferrari 550 575m 6.0L race engine availability

engineYears.h2: Ferrari 550 575m Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Ferrari 550 and 575m production timeline (1996-2006), organised so owners can quickly identify their F133 engine code and compare rebuilt prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The 550 Maranello debuted Ferrari's modern front-engined V12 with the all-new F133A engine, marking a return to grand touring excellence after the Testarossa era.
keyChanges: Launch of Ferrari 550 Maranello with F133A 5.5L V12 (485hp)
Aluminium block with Nikasil cylinder liners for weight reduction
Bosch Motronic ME5.2 engine management system introduced
knownFor: Hydraulic timing chain tensioners prone to collapse on pre-1999 units
Cylinder head cracking at exhaust ports emerges as key failure mode
Highly sought after for classic GT driving; values rising steadily
years.cta: Get quotes for 1996-1998 Ferrari 550 575m replacement engines
years.ticker: Ferrari 550 cylinder head crack repair • Ferrari 550 timing chain tensioner upgrade • F133A engine replacement cost UK

years.description: Mid-production updates improved F133A reliability; the limited-run 550 Barchetta introduced the detuned F133E calibration for open-top driving.
keyChanges: Revised mechanical timing chain tensioners introduced for 1999+ 550 Maranello
Launch of 550 Barchetta Pininfarina (2000-2001) with F133E detuned calibration
Final 550 Maranello production year (2001) represents peak F133A refinement
knownFor: 1999+ tensioner upgrades significantly improve long-term reliability
Barchetta F133E shares head cracking vulnerability but benefits from lower stress calibration
Low-mileage Barchetta examples command significant premium in replacement market
years.cta: Get quotes for 1999-2001 Ferrari 550 575m replacement engines
years.ticker: Ferrari 550 Barchetta engine replacement • F133E calibration specialist rebuild • 550 low mileage donor engine UK

years.description: The 575M Maranello introduced the enlarged F133B 5.7L V12 with revised oiling, updated ECU mapping and optional F1 automated manual transmission.
keyChanges: Launch of 575M Maranello with F133B 5.7L V12 (500-515hp)
Introduction of F1 automated manual transmission option with paddle-shift calibration
575M GTC race variant debuts with dry-sump F133C lubrication system
knownFor: F133B oil pump drive gears susceptible to shear under sustained high-RPM use
Camshaft variator bronze bushing wear emerges as common 575M issue per service bulletin
GTC F133C requires specialist dry-sump maintenance knowledge for rebuilds
years.cta: Get quotes for 2002-2003 Ferrari 550 575m replacement engines

faq.h2: Ferrari 550 575m Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Ferrari 550 575m engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Rebuilt Ferrari 550 F133A engines typically cost £12,000-£13,000, while 575M F133B units range £13,800-£14,500 supply-only in the UK. Prices include VAT, 12-month warranty and nationwide delivery; supply & fit adds £2,800-£4,500 depending on location and variant complexity.
keyPoints: F133A 5.5L rebuilt: £12,000-£13,000 supply only
F133B 5.7L rebuilt: £13,800-£14,500 supply only
Core exchange can reduce cost by approximately £1,200
Superamerica and GTC variants command premium due to rarity
items.cta: Compare rebuilt F133 engine prices for your Ferrari 550 575m

items.question: What causes cylinder head cracking on Ferrari 550 575m engines?
items.answer: Cylinder head cracking on F133 engines occurs at exhaust ports due to thermal cycling stress; exacerbated by aggressive driving, missed coolant service or inadequate warm-up procedures on high-performance V12s.
keyPoints: Cracks typically form at exhaust port junctions on cylinder heads
Dye-penetrant testing is essential during any F133 rebuild
MLS gasket upgrades help prevent recurrence after repair
Rebuilt engines include crack-tested heads as standard
items.cta: Get quotes for Ferrari 550 575m cylinder head crack repair or replacement

items.question: Is the Ferrari 575M timing chain failure preventable?
items.answer: Yes - hydraulic timing chain tensioners on F133B engines can be upgraded to mechanical kits at 60k miles, preventing chain skip on this interference engine before catastrophic valve damage occurs.
keyPoints: Hydraulic tensioners lose pressure with age; mechanical kits are permanent solution
Chain rattle at idle is early warning sign requiring immediate inspection
Rebuilt F133 engines include upgraded mechanical tensioners pre-fitted
60k mile service interval critical for preventative maintenance
items.cta: Find a Ferrari 575M specialist for timing chain tensioner upgrade

items.question: Can I fit a 575M F133B engine into a 550 Maranello chassis?
items.answer: Ferrari explicitly prohibits F133B-to-F133A swaps due to ECU wiring harness, immobiliser pairing and bellhousing differences; only matched engine codes should be used for replacement to ensure plug-and-play compatibility.
keyPoints: F133A uses Bosch ME5.2 ECU; F133B uses ME7.3 with different calibration
Wiring harness and immobiliser systems are not cross-compatible
Bellhousing bolt patterns differ between 550 and 575M transmissions
EngineMarket only supplies code-matched replacements to avoid installation issues
items.cta: Get quotes for code-matched Ferrari 550 575m engine replacement

items.question: What warranty comes with a rebuilt Ferrari 550 575m engine?
items.answer: All rebuilt F133 engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty covering internal components, timing system and oiling circuit, excluding improper installation or pre-existing faults.
keyPoints: 12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard on all rebuilt units
Coverage includes internal components, timing system and oiling circuit
Warranty excludes improper installation, pre-existing faults or misuse
Specialist installation documentation recommended for warranty validation
items.cta: Compare Ferrari 550 575m rebuilt engine warranty terms from UK specialists

items.question: How long does a rebuilt Ferrari F133 V12 engine last?
items.answer: With proper maintenance using 10W-60 full synthetic oil and 6,000-mile change intervals, rebuilt F133 engines typically last 80,000+ miles; longevity depends on driving style, service adherence and initial rebuild quality.
keyPoints: 10W-60 full synthetic oil mandatory per Ferrari specification
6,000-mile oil change intervals critical for Nikasil liner longevity
Mechanical timing tensioners and billet oil pump upgrades extend service life
Bench dyno validation during rebuild ensures baseline performance and reliability
items.cta: Enquire about Ferrari F133 rebuild longevity and maintenance schedule

items.question: Do you offer core exchange for Ferrari 550 575m engine replacements?
items.answer: Yes - core exchange provides approximately £1,200 credit toward your rebuilt F133 engine; your old long-block must be complete with no cracked block, spun bearings or catastrophic damage to qualify.
keyPoints: Core credit approximately £1,200 applied to rebuilt engine invoice
Core must be complete long-block with no catastrophic damage
Core return shipping arranged via insured engine transport service
Inspection criteria applied before credit is released to customer

items.question: How long does Ferrari 550 575m engine delivery take in the UK?
items.answer: Rebuilt F133 engines are typically delivered within 3-5 working days to mainland UK with full insurance; core collection service available simultaneously to streamline the replacement process.
keyPoints: 3-5 working day delivery to mainland UK standard
Fully insured engine transport included in quoted price
Core collection service can be scheduled concurrently with delivery
London, Manchester and Birmingham specialist fitters offer next-day collection
items.cta: Check Ferrari 550 575m engine delivery availability for your location

items.question: Are Ferrari 550 575m engines interference design?
items.answer: Yes - all F133 engines are interference design; timing chain failure causes immediate valve-piston contact and catastrophic engine damage, making 60k-mile chain service critical for prevention.
keyPoints: F133 is interference design; valve and piston occupy same space at TDC
Timing chain skip or breakage causes immediate valve-piston contact
Mechanical tensioner upgrades at 60k miles prevent catastrophic failure
Compression testing essential after any suspected timing event
items.cta: Get quotes for Ferrari 550 575m timing chain preventative maintenance

items.question: What fuel does a Ferrari 550 575m F133 engine require?
items.answer: F133 engines require minimum 98 RON premium unleaded petrol; using lower octane fuel may trigger knock sensors, reduce performance and potentially cause long-term damage to high-compression V12 components.
keyPoints: Minimum 98 RON premium unleaded petrol required per Ferrari specification
Lower octane fuel triggers knock sensors and reduces power output
UK E10 fuel compatibility requires ethanol-resistant sensor upgrades on late F133B/D
Fuchs Titan recommended oil specification supports high-performance fuel requirements
items.cta: Find Ferrari 550 575m specialists familiar with UK fuel compatibility requirements
